title: RP Campfire
description: Campfires gives regeneration and protection against monsters by consuming wood.
body:
Campfires are now a powerfull tool. Regeneration over time, a few recipes, and a protection against monsters. Useful for UHC or just RP gameplay. ## Main features - Add **Fire Starter** recipe: 1 stick + 1 stick. Hold **right-click** to use on a campfire to try to ignite it. Then, when smoky (last 15s), quickly drop flamable items on it. - Throwing **logs/planks/sticks or flammable things** into a campfire will make it stronger and more durable - Add 3 recipes to campfires (stick to torch, log to charcoal, rotten flesh to leather) - When fuelled, gives a regeneration effect in a short range (depending of the fire level) - When fuelled, accelerate cooking times on campfires (depending of the fire level) - When fuelled, affraid nearby monsters (apply slowness effect) (depending of the fire level) - dying campfire now enter a **smoking state** for 300 seconds (default). Can be revive with fuel (no flint&steel/firestarter required). - Custom particles depending of the fire level ## Datapack's Options In game, use: ```/function fkcpf:options/get```

Patreon Version The free versions are designed to be fun and fully playable. The Patreon version is for players and server owners who want more control, comfort, and flexibility through an in-game settings menu to tweak the parameters live, without file editing. Becoming a Gold Patreon also means: - Getting access to every Patreon versions of every projects. - Getting your name listed in each project and on this page (see above). - Supporting long-term development, updates, and new features 💛
Mod Version Mod packaging is designed to be more convenient for your installations. Executed on the server side, it does not need to be installed on the client side. Keep in mind that inside the mod is simply a datapack. In addition, interactions between too many mods can degrade the functioning of one or more mods.
Issue / Bug This pack require entities (marker) on scoreboard: you might have to turn it on if using Spigot/Paper.
